observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer, as the Property and Supplies
Officer, agreed to verify the existence of these PPE items to determine
whether to fully depreciate the PPE or not.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries to rectify the unrecorded depreciation expense of
the subject PPE items.

Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize
annual depreciation for all items of PPE in accordance with the IPSAS and
COA issuances.

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the completion of the
one-time PPE cleansing in full compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 by
assigning dedicated personnel and temporarily relieving the members of the
Inventory Committee from their regular duties to ensure the timely execution of
all required procedures and to immediately reconcile the results of the physical
count with accounting and property records.

We further recommended and Management also agreed that upon
completion of the conduct of inventory and preparation of the reports, the
Municipal Mayor file a request for authority to derecognize non-
existing/missing PPEs to the audit team, and support its request with the
certified lists required in Section 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006, to take
full advantage of the one-time cleansing privilege afforded by the
Commission and establish accuracy of its PPE balances reported in the
financial statements.

Lastly, we recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to adopt the
uniform numbering system for property numbers of PPE and require the
Property Unit to update the property stickers based on the prescribed format
and to include the required information under the said COA Circular.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to, henceforth,
include the required disclosures for the Road Network account as required
under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting entries to accurately reflect the correct amounts of RPT
and SET receivables.

We further recommended that, henceforth, at the beginning of the year, the
Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure timely submission of the Certified List
of Taxpayers, with corresponding amounts due and collectible for the period,
so the Municipal Accountant can prepare the Journal Entry Voucher to
record the debit to RPT Receivable/SET Receivable and credit the
corresponding Deferred RPT Income/SET Income, which would be adjusted
during the year, upon collection from taxpayers.

We recommended and Management agreed to install internal control
mechanisms by using the RIS to request supplies and materials from stock
and to consolidate them in the SSMI, which shall be submitted to the
Municipal Accountant for recording in the appropriate expense accounts for
the supplies consumed.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of
recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to expense accounts
and henceforth, record purchases and utilization of supplies and materials in
accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and
Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to fairly
present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to establish internal
accounting policies to identify whether a particular expenditure pertains to
Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses, Training Expenses,
Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses, etc.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to,
henceforth, strictly follow the Revised Chart of Accounts, as required in COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed, in
coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant, to
ensure that annual appropriation and disbursement of funds for discretionary
purposes are within the statutory limit provided for in Section 325(h) of RA
No. 7160.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed, in coordination with
the Municipal Treasurer, to trace the discrepancy in the General Fund to
previous bank statements and credit/debit memos to identify appropriate
reconciling items. Furthermore, we recommended and the Municipal
Accountant also agreed, with assistance from the Municipal Treasurer, to
secure the bank statement for Account No. 0292-0035-28, to reconcile the
discrepancy in the Trust Fund.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to issue final
demand letters directing the individuals involved to immediately liquidate all
outstanding cash advances. If no liquidation is made, we recommend that the
Municipal Accountant impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of
those who still fail to settle their accounts after receiving notice.

We further recommended and Management agreed to take aggressive
measures to compel all accountable officers to settle their accounts, including
the filing of appropriate administrative proceedings, pursuant to CSC
Memorandum Circular No. 12, s. 2012.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to strictly comply with
Section 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, by ensuring the regular
consolidation of the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) into the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submitting the same to the
Municipal Accountant in a timely manner.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a
thorough reconciliation of inventory balances and record the corresponding
journal entries to reflect the correct financial position and performance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that semi-
expendable properties are properly recorded under the appropriate Semi-
Expendable Inventory accounts, rather than under Other Supplies and
Materials Expenses. Proper accounting treatment must be observed by
preparing the required Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) and utilizing the
prescribed new accounts and illustrative entries found in Annexes B and C
of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and Property/Supply
Office agreed to ensure that Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) are issued to
end users upon the receipt of semi-expendable items to establish
accountability, as required under Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2024-
006. The Property Unit must also maintain the complete set of prescribed
forms, registries, and reports, such as the:
10.9.1 Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI)
10.9.2 Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI)
10.9.3 Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC)
10.9.4 Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (SPLC)
10.9.5 And other related forms outlined in Section 4.7 of COA Circular No.
2022-004

Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to strengthen internal
controls by institutionalizing the required inventory monitoring and
reporting processes, assigning specific personnel to maintain the necessary
records, and conducting regular training and orientation on COA Circular
Nos. 2022-004 and 2024-006 to ensure uniform understanding and
implementation.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prepare the
RPCRNS and reconcile it with the records available in the Office of the
Accountant. We also recommend that the GSO or property custodian submit
the Report on LRN as required under COA Circular No. 2015-008.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, secure the
Certificate of Availability of Funds (CAF) from the Municipal Accountant,
prior to entering into any contract and before making any disbursement or
obligations charged to any authorized appropriation, in accordance with
Section 86 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that all
purchases are supported by the appropriate and complete documentation,
pursuant to PD No. 1445 and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, as amended
by RA 12009.

We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, ensure that
annual appropriations and disbursements for discretionary purposes or
those relating to Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses do not exceed
the statutory threshold as provided in Section 325(h) of the RA No. 7160.

We further recommended and Management agreed that meals and snacks
served during regular and administrative meetings of local officials and
employees be charged to the appropriations for EME, to the extent of its
threshold, and representation allowances of officials concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. enjoin the Sanggunian to set aside an appropriation for the installation of the electronic New Government Accounting System (e-NGAS), which is made available by the NGAS. 
2. require the Municipal Accountant to submit a complete set of FS and TB, with pertinent schedules and reports which must reconcile with the amounts reported on the FS; and
3. require the Municipal Accountant to support the FS submission with the appropriate working files on the adjustments and entries made, which affected the changes in the amounts reported.
observations.managementAction:
Management still had to wait for the availability of funds for the proper appropriation of funds for the installation of e-NGAS.
The Municipal Accountant had already submitted the complete set of FS and TB only on May 29, 2024, which was beyond the set deadline.
The working files submitted were incomplete for the audit team to verify and validate.

observations.recommendation:
4. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly BRS pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
5. and consequently, draw the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Cash in Bank-Local Currency Current (1-01-02-010) and Cash in Bank-Local Currency Savings (1-01-02-020).
6. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for early detection and correction of adjustments and errors, if any, pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
The monthly BRS had only been submitted on May 31, 2024. During the cash exam December 13, 2024, the monthly BRS still had not been prepared by the Municipal Accountant because they were still completing their entries for their transactions and the preparation of the financial statements.
Due to the delayed preparation of the monthly BRS, reconciling items and discrepancies had not been verified for the Office to draw the necessary entries.
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer had been coordinating with each other in reconciling the cash balances.

observations.recommendation:
7. We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to, henceforth, ensure that all cash advances are supported by the complete documentary requirements pursuant to PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
8. and immediately disapprove cash advances without the necessary supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer promised that, henceforth, all cash advances shall be supported by complete documentary requirements.
And immediately disapprove cash advances without the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor order the withholding of payment of any money due from officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances, 30 days after the service of the demand letters where no liquidation has been submitted by the accountable officers concerned, pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002.
12. We further recommended that Management, henceforth, desist from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers with unliquidated balances.
13. Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to impose sanctions on the concerned erring officials and employees, in accordance with PD 1445 and the revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction:
The Accountant had not sent demand letters to the concerned officials and employees.
Additional cash advances had not been granted to those with unliquidated balances.
Management had not implemented the imposition of sanctions. Demand letters would need to be sent before imposing the said sanctions.

observations.recommendation:
14. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create a PPE Inventory Committee in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
15. Prepare and submit the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for the conduct of a physical count of PPE of the LGU; and
16. Expedite its procedures in the conduct of physical count and submit within three months or less the required reports such as the RCPPE, ICF, List of PPEs found at Station and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs, PIP.
17. We also recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant collaborate with the PPE Inventory Committee to ensure that all PPEs included in the RCPPE are duly reconciled with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction:
Government already created an Inventory Committee which started to conduct the physical count of its properties.
The Office of the Municipal Accountant promised to coordinate with the PPE Inventory Committee once the physical count of its properties is completed.

observations.recommendation:
18. We recommended that Management evaluate the current composition of the LDRRMO and assess whether there is a need to complete the mandatory positions of the said Office. If the necessity results in the benefits exceeding the costs of filling-up the positions.
19. making representation with the Sanggunian for the allocation of the necessary budget and staffing, in accordance with the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; and
20. prioritizing the hiring of qualified personnel to fill in the vacant plantilla positions to assure the implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.
observations.managementAction:
Management evaluated the composition of the LDRRMO and found that even if there were limitations in the accomplishments of the Office, the current condition can still work effectively and provide the necessary outputs.
The Municipal Mayor explained that this was still not a priority expenditure of the Municipality in CY 2023.
Management had not prioritized staffing and hiring during the audited year.

observations.recommendation:
21. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) require the personnel concerned or the end-user duly authorized by the Municipality, as Head of the Procuring Entity, to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.
22. We also recommend that the BAC should refrain from awarding a contract unless the RFQ contains the required details of the goods procured.
observations.managementAction: The BAC required the personnel concerned to appropriately fill up the relevant data and information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996. However, there will still transactions that were not verified by the BAC, with regard to the completeness of specifications indicated in the returned RFQs.

observations.recommendation: 24. We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer and the BAC Chairman, henceforth, use the latest DPRI as the ceiling price in the procurement of drugs and medicines in compliance with RA 9502 and DOH AO No. 2016-0034 dated August 9, 2016. Otherwise, the drugs and medicines procured were included in the PNF. For those that weren't, the appropriate certification was secured to support the procurement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer explained that her office used the price range provided for Geographically Isolated and Disadvantaged Areas, because these allow a higher range than that of the DPRI, and the responsive suppliers had higher drug prices. However, she promised to henceforth implement the reference to the DPRI, as recommended.

observations.recommendation: 27. We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that purchases pertaining to the acquisition of motor vehicles be supported with the appropriate and complete documentation, pursuant to PD No. 1445, DBM BC No. 2022-1, and DILG MC No. 2022-105.
observations.managementAction: BAC assured the Audit Team that before processing the procurement of motor vehicles, the appropriate APMV shall be secured. Management explained that there were still purchases that were not supported with the appropriate APMV because these transactions happened before the dissemination of the advice of the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
28. We recommended that the BAC use Shopping as the mode of procurement only when it satisfies the requirements of Section 52.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
29. We also recommended that the BAC, when recommending approval for alternative modes of procurement, it should indicate the specific reason/justification for using such mode for each purchase, instead of providing a uniform justification for all purchases made which did not undergo competitive bidding.
observations.managementAction: The BAC assured the Audit Team that they will implement the audit team’s recommendation. In CY 2023, they honestly neglected to impose these recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
30. We recommended that the LDRRM Council ensure that the 70% budget allocation for disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness is optimally utilized in order to better prepare the Municipality for any calamities which may happen in the future.
31. We further recommended that the LDRRM Council ensure that the programs and projects included in its LDRRM Plan and AIP are realistic and attainable.
observations.managementAction: Management assured that they will be monitoring the utilization of the fund and would evaluate the projects, programs, and activities charged thereto. In CY 2023, they implemented some projects, but only a minimal percentage was utilized because of priorities and availability of manpower.
Management had not taken any action due to different priorities. Draft PSCP prepared; working group not yet created; actions listed above were not undertaken during the audited year.

observations.recommendation: 40. We recommended and BAC agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee use Shopping as the mode of procurement only when it satisfies the requirements of Section 52.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The BAC promised to henceforth strictly follow the conditions when using “Shopping” as an alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation:
45. We recommended and Management agreed to, henceforth, demand from the organization the issuance of AF No. 51 for payments made to the organization, pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and other pertinent laws and issuances.
46. We further recommended that the representative from the Office of the Municipal Treasurer agreed to submit the lacking ORs or documents to reconcile with the ₱20,000.00 discrepancy.
observations.managementAction:
Despite the demands of the organization, the available proof of receipt of the PCL was only the VAT ORs registered with the BIR.
Management neglected to take any action to reconcile the discrepancy.
Management had not taken any action because this was not among the priority programs in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: 51. We recommended that the GFPS prepare and set up the GAD Agenda of the Municipal Government and identify priority gender issues of the LGU for the next three-year term of leadership, pursuant to Section 4, Item C.2 (1) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1, as amended.
observations.managementAction: Management had not taken any action because this was not among the priority programs in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: 52. We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of real property taxes and avail of the remedies provided under Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code, especially for those with delinquent accounts of more than two years.
observations.managementAction: It was not among the priority expenditures in the next calendar year. Management assured that remedies would be used to increase the collection of taxes, going forward.

observations.recommendation: 53. We recommended that Management reimburse from the General Fund the LDRRMF for the amount of expenses not chargeable thereto. We further recommended that the MDRRMO use the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects, programs and activities in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
observations.managementAction: It was not among the priority expenditures in CY 2023. The MDRRMO agreed to use the LDRRMF for disaster-related projects, programs and activities in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.

observations.recommendation: 55. We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) establish the GAD database as storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1 to ensure that the PPAs are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: Management engaged a contractor who can assist the Municipality in establishing the database. This is still an ongoing process.

observations.recommendation: 56. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in accordance with Section 98 of P.D. 1445: a) conduct a periodic review of all outstanding payables; and b) validate available documents and revert those which had remained outstanding for more than two years for which no administrative or judicial claim had been filed in accordance with Section 98 of P.D.1445.
observations.managementAction: Conducted a periodic review of all outstanding payables. Validated available documents; however, not all payables could be validated due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: 57. We recommended that Management prepare a 5-Year Business Plan and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass an ordinance recognizing the continued operations of the existing LEEs in accordance with the LEE Manual as prescribed in Local Budget Circular No. 111 dated June 10, 2016 to ensure the efficient and effective provision of basic services and facilities to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management had not organized a team to prepare the 5-Year Business Plan because it was not among the priority programs/activities during the audited year.

observations.recommendation: 59. We recommended that the concerned officials and employees refund the amount paid as CNA incentive.
observations.managementAction: Refund is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 60. We recommended that the concerned officials and employees refund the PBB paid to them.
observations.managementAction: Refund is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 61. We recommended that the municipal officials and employees refund the excess PS amounting to ₱1,380,159.73 and, henceforth, adhere strictly to the PS limitation pursuant to Section 325 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The PS Limitation had been observed by the Municipality. Refund of the balance of the excess PS is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: 64. We recommended that the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer dispose of the unserviceable motor vehicles and transportation equipment in accordance with Section 70 of PD 1445 and Section 125 of the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The inventory had not been completed nor reports were accomplished.

observations.recommendation: 65. Hasten the acquisition of a biometric system of recording attendance to ensure that the times of arrival and departure of officials and employees are properly recorded and monitored.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had been using Bundy Clocks. Using a biometric system would entail more budget for its acquisition and installation, which was not among the priority expenditures of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: 66. We recommended that Management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2013 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials were demanded to refund the overpayment of PEI. But, some officials had not fully refunded their balances.

observations.recommendation:
67. Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of the Waterworks System; and
68. Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of these economic enterprises in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and minimize costs/expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management shall: Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of the Waterworks System; and Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of these economic enterprises in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and minimize costs/expenses.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory
Committee agreed to complete the reports necessary to establish the correct
balances of PPE items that should be reported in the financial statements.

1.10 We further recommended and Management agreed to temporarily relieve the
Inventory Committee from their regular duties during the one-time cleansing
per Sec. 5.3 of COA Circular 2020-006.

1.11 Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to file a request
for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs to the audit team, and
support its request with the certified lists required in Section 8.3 of COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to take full advantage of the one-time cleansing
privilege afforded by the Commission and establish accuracy of its PPE
balances reported in the financial statements.

2.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revise the Notes to
the Financial Statements to include the principal amount, interest rate,
maturity date, amount for each purpose, and interest expense of the Loans
Payable—Domestic account to properly inform the users of the financial
statements about the municipal government's financial performance,
particularly in financial management.

3.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and
submit to the Audit Team the monthly BRS pursuant to Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

3.12 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective
records for early detection and correction of adjustments and errors, if any,
pursuant to Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445.

3.13 Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to trace the discrepancies noted, and reconcile the Cash-in-
Bank account, as presented in the financial statements against the amounts
reported in the bank statements.

4.8 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to, henceforth:
4.8.1 Require the outgoing AO to submit their cashbook to the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant for verification and recording purposes;
4.8.2 Instruct the newly appointed or designated AO to use a new cashbook in
accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and
4.8.3 Brief the newly appointed or designated AOs on the proper recording of
transactions and other matters relating to their role as a disbursing officer.

5.12 We recommended and the BAC agreed to use the Shopping mode for
procurement only when it satisfies the stipulated requirements of Section 52.1 of
the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

5.13 We also recommended and the BAC agreed to specify the reason/justification
for using an alternative mode of procurement for each purchase, instead of
providing a general justification for all purchases that did not undergo
competitive bidding when recommending approval for alternative modes of
procurement.

6.8 We recommended and the OIC Municipal Health Officer and the BAC
Chairman, henceforth, use the latest DPRI as the ceiling price in the
procurement of drugs and medicines in compliance with RA 9502 and DOH AO
No. 2019-0040 dated October 14, 2019.

6.9 The OIC Municipal Health Officer explained that she referred the prices of the
drugs and medicines to the Geographically Isolated and Disadvantaged Areas
(GIDA) range. She further clarified if the Municipality could refer to the price range
provided for GIDA since some of the medicines sold by the responsive suppliers
are more expensive than the DPRI. However, she assured the Audit Team that they
would refer to the latest DPRI going forward.

7.8 We recommended, and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed, that complete
detailed specifications be indicated in the PO before proceeding with the
delivery of drugs and medicines.

7.9 We also recommended and the personnel concerned or the end-user duly
authorized by the Municipal Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, agreed
to fill up the relevant and required information in the PO pursuant to Item B of
COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

8.11 We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to, henceforth,
first secure the appropriate authority to purchase motor vehicles, in
compliance with DBM BC No. 2022-1.

8.12 We further recommended and Management agreed to submit an explanation
for the procurement of the surplus vehicle to justify the violation of Section 9 of
BC No. 2022-1 prohibiting the purchase of reconditioned motor vehicles.

9.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
9.9.1 Include in its annual budget the appropriation for the establishment of
IAS; and
9.9.2 Facilitate the creation of an IAS in the Municipality in accordance with
AO No. 70 and RA No. 3456.

9.10 We also recommended that the IAS use the 2020 Revised PGIAM in the
exercise of their internal audit functions.

10.15 We recommended and the Municipal Assessor agreed to:
10.15.1 Coordinate with the Provincial Assessor in the preparation of the
updated SMV of the Municipality pursuant to Section 212 of RA No.
7160; and
10.15.2 Initiate the immediate conduct of a general revision of real property
assessments after the enactment of the updated SMVs and ensure that this
is done every three years as required under Section 219 of RA No. 7160
and DILG-DOF JMC No. 2010-001.

11.10 We recommended and the Municipal Engineer, in coordination with the
Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, agreed to evaluate the
feasibility and closely monitor the implementation of the various PPAs in
accordance with the approved development plan as required under Section
476 (4) of R.A. No. 7160.

11.11 In addition, we recommended and Management agreed to consider all
possible limitations, including the availability of the deed of donation,
equipment, and manpower during the planning stage.

12.10 We recommended, and the LDRRM Council agreed, that the 70 per cent
budget allocation for disaster prevention, mitigation, and preparedness be
optimally utilized to better prepare the Municipality for future calamities.

12.11 We further recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to ensure that the
programs and projects included in its LDRRM Plan and AIP are realistic and
attainable.

9. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the cash accountabilities of AOs are in accordance with their existing bonds, pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
10. We also recommended that Management review and evaluate the maximum cash accountabilities of AOs and apply for an increase in the amount of bond, when necessary, in accordance with the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
The cash accountabilities of the AOs had been within their existing bonds.
Management reviewed and evaluated the maximum cash accountabilities of AO and ensured that each was within their existing bonds.

observations.recommendation: 23. We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that all purchases be supported by the appropriate and complete documentation, pursuant to PD No. 1445 and the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The procurement of goods, materials, and supplies were supported with the required documentary support.

observations.recommendation:
25. We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms by using the RIS to request and issue the supplies and material in stock and to consolidate the same in the SSMI, which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the appropriate expense accounts for the supplies consumed.
26. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recording purchases of supplies and materials directly to expense accounts and henceforth, record purchases and utilization of supplies and materials in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and Section IV of COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014, to fairly present the expense and inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Supplies and materials were consolidated in the Summary of supplies and materials consumed.
The perpetual inventory method had been used in recording supplies and materials.

observations.recommendation: 32. We recommended and the GAD Focal Person submit a written explanation on the non-submission of the GPB and AR for CY 2022 and henceforth, strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014, for the timely submission of the GPB and the corresponding AR.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person submitted the CY 2022 GPB and AR.

observations.recommendation:
41. We recommended that the BAC conduct competitive bidding for each procurement, in accordance with the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184, except as provided in Rule XVI, to ensure transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.
42. We furthermore recommended that Management, henceforth, stop approving procurement activities that are not included in their APP.
observations.managementAction: Only those procurements included in the APP had been processed by BAC.

observations.recommendation:
43. We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), henceforth, ensure that drugs and medicines procured are included in the PNF Volume I, 8th Edition (2019), pursuant to DOH Administrative Order No. 2016-0034 dated August 9, 2016, otherwise, the appropriate approval of exemption request should support the procurement.
44. We also recommended that the BAC, henceforth, require the certification from the requisitioning officer or by duly authorized officer that the products being requisitioned and procured fall within and conform with the PNF Essential Medicines List to support the Purchase Request, in accordance with the aforementioned DOH Administrative Order.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensured that the drugs and medicines procured were included in the PNF. For those that weren’t, the appropriate certification was secured and attached to the corresponding disbursements.

observations.recommendation: 54. We recommended that Management hasten the implementation of the livelihood programs pursuant to the MOA between the Municipality and the DOLE. Otherwise, request for revision of the MOA or return the funds to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Management hastened the implementation of the livelihood programs pursuant to the MOA between the Municipality and the DOLE. observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) agreed to
require the personnel concerned or the end-use duly authorized by the Municipal
Mayor, as Head of the Procuring Entity, to fill up the relevant and required
information in the PO pursuant to Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated
August 15, 1996. We also recommended that the BAC should refrain from
awarding a contract when the RFQ does not contain specific details of the goods
procured.

We recommended that Management and the BAC examine thoroughly the
documents submitted by the suppliers to ensure that contracts are awarded to
suppliers with financial capability and goods delivered to the Municipality are
reasonably priced.

Management asserted that with the COVID-19 pandemic, each household in the
locality secured their own goods, hence, the supply of local basic commodities,
esspecially rice, was limited. Management had to find a supplier which can deliver the
needed supplies of rice before payment was made. During the bidding, only Connil and
San Enterprises responded to the urgent need fo the Municipality. The BAC thought
that they were financially capable because they were able to respond and deliver the
needs of the Municipality. However, Management assured the Audit Team that the
BAC will religiously adhere to the audit recommendations and, henceforth, ensure that
contracts are awarded to financially capable suppliers.

We recommended that the BAC properly plan the procurement of its
projects and refrain from splitting of requisitions, purchase orders, vouchers and
others pursuant to COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976.

The BAC Chairman explained that there were several requistions because
project implementation was not done simultaneously due to lack of personnel in the
Office of the Municipal Engineer. Also, the purchase of materials was prepared per
project to avoid expiration and losses. However, the BAC Chairman also added that
the BAC is committed to abide by the recommendations of the Audit Team.

The Audit Team recognizes the lack of manpower in the Office of the Municipal
Engineer. However, this should not prevent Management from planning the
procurement of its projects to be able to come up with a consolidated Annual
Procurement Plan (APP) and determine those which can be procured at the same time
thru competitive public bidding. With regard to expiration and losses of construction
materials, it can be addressed by specifying the delivery terms in the PO.

We recommended that the BAC resort to shopping as a mode of
procurement only when it satisfies the requirements of Section 52.1 of the Revised
IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

The BAC assured the Audit Team that it is committed to abide by the audit
recommendations and will strictly implement and follow the rules and procedures of
the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to come up with right decisions and actions.

We recommended that Management explain the deficiencies noted in the
accounting for the one-unit garbage compactor purchased. We further
recommended that Management strictly adhere to the accounting policies for
properties as provided in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

Management explained/commented the following for the observations noted:
a) It has been the practice of the Municipality that before an item is posted for
public bidding, the Municipal Treasurer would issue a certification that
funds are available. A complete set of documents would be attached to the
DV for processing, so that the ObR would be signed by the Muncipal
Budget Officer only before the DV would be processed in the Offices of the
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Mayor;
b) The date of receipt of the PO and other necessary details will be indicated;
c) The AIR was already prepared but pending signature after the actual
inspection of the garbage compactor and the check was prepared after all
the documents were complete. Actual payment was only made by the
Municipal Treasurer on December 16, 2020, after the said unit was
inspected and accepted, as evidenced by the logbook of released checks;
d) The JEV was dated the same as the DV as by practice, and a journal entry
was made immediately after the DV was processed in the accounting office.
It was supposed that nothing was wrong with such practice since the Office
of the Municipal Accountant can easily correct the entry; and
e) In the usual practice of the Municipality, the RIS is a standard form that the
end-user will prepare as part of the supporting documents to the DV while
the ARE is issued by the Municipal Treasurer to the end user (1st copy), and
the 2nd copy is kept by her as the property custodian, hence, no ARE was
attached to the DV.

Nevertheless, Management informed the Audit Team that they will comply
with the recommendations and for the BAC to include the necessary information in the
PO, pursuant to existing accounting rules and regulations.

The Audit Team appreciates that Management sees the audit observations as a
help to improve their internal control. However, regarding the issuance of RIS, it is
emphasized that it is a form used for requisitions of supplies carried in stock, and is not
required for the issuance of property and equipment to the end-user, as it is covered by
the ARE (now Property Acknowledgement Receipt or PAR pursuant to Sec. 58 of the
Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II). A copy of
the PAR shall be attached to the DV, instead of the RIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to undertake the following courses of action:

• Create an Inventory Committee and see to it that a physical count of
properties is conducted every year with the report to be submitted to the
Auditor on or before January 31 of the following year pursuant to
Section 156 of COA Circular No. 92-386 and Section 124 of the Manual
on the NGAS, Volume I;
• Require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and Office of the
Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and ledger cards for
each PPE item and reconcile both records with the physical count; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments
for all valid variances noted.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to submit the Report on
Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF on or before the 15th day of the
following month and include in the report all information required in Annex B
of COA Circular No. 2012-02 to enable the concerned offices to conduct timely
evaluation of the LDRRMF sources and utilization.

observations.managementAction: Supporting documents have already been submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Documents are still for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant – Designate to maintain their respective property records for each real property and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to prioritize the filling of application for titling of all real properties of the municipal government so that transfer certificates of title in favour of the municipality of Basay can be issued to establish the municipality’s ownership thereto. For real property acquired prior to the creation of the municipality and for which records are no longer available, coordinate with the Provincial Register of Deeds and the Provincial Attorney’s Office for guidance in locating or applying for titles thereto.
observations.managementAction: Maintenance of property records has been started and is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to do the job.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that municipal officials and employees refund the excess Personal Serices amounting to ₱1,380,159.73 and henceforth, adhere strictly to the PS limitation pursuant to Section 325 of Republic Act No. 7160.
observations.reason: Awaiting issuance of the Notice of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to comply with the requirements for payment of PBB to officials and employees, otherwise, such payment shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.reason: No basis for granting the PBB.

observations.recommendation: Hasten the acquisition of a biometric system of recording attendance to ensure that the times of arrival and departure of officials and employees are properly recorded and monitored.
observations.reason: For consideration in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should: a. Design/adopt measures and strategies to remedy the high expenditures in the operations of the Waterworks System; and b. Review and revisit the Plans and Targets in the operations of these economic enterprises in order to address the areas where the municipal government can generate additional income and minimize costs/expenses.
observations.reason: For consideration in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee to study and explore possibilities of increasing local revenues in order to minimize the municipality’s dependence on external revenue allotments to finance its operations. Recommend for the revision of the local tax code ordinance to increase new tax rates which are more suitable to current economic trends and instruct the municipal treasurer to intensify real property tax collections to provide additional sources of revenue.
observations.reason: For consideration by the new Municipal Treasurer who just assumed office on April 1, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to utilize the 20% EDF in accordance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that the amount paid as CNA incentive be refunded by officials and employees upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed that PBB paid to officials and employees be refunded upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC refrain from specifying brand names in any procurement activity in accordance with Section 18 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 to allow wide competition by prospective bidders.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that disbursements charged to AICS be supported with the required documents listed in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15, series of 2014.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to appropriate one percent of its IRA and utilize the same for implementation of PPAs determined and identified by the LCPC that would safeguard and protect the welfare of children in accordance with Section 15 of Republic Act No. 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended that management adopt measures to mitigate the effects of the current open dump site, concretize methods and procedures for the phaseout/eventual closure of the current open dump site within a specific time frame and make robust its compliance with Republic Act 9003.
observations.managementAction: Initial discussions were made by the Municipality and the City Government of Bayawan to dump its residual waste to the latter’s sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Awaiting finality of the agreement between the Municipality and the City Government of Bayawan to dump its residual waste to the latter’s sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to submit copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders/job orders and the related supporting documents within five (5) working days from its execution in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: Submission of said documents were made during the first months after the recommendation was issued. However, the submission during the later part of the year 2016 has not been faithfully complied with.
observations.reason: Submission during the later part of the year 2016 has not been faithfully complied with.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Treasurer agreed not to deduct from the salaries of municipal officials and employees any amount for the payment of their personal loans with institutions not authorized under Section 48 of the General Provisions of the FY 2015 General Appropriations Act.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has existing MOAs with these cooperatives. Thus, they still continue the salary deductions for the loan amortizations. However, the LGU no longer enter into a new MOA with cooperatives in 2016.
observations.reason: Has existing MOAs with these cooperatives.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to increase the fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer concerned to an amount sufficient to cover the already maximum accountability for a given period, in accordance with the revised schedule of bond preium rates provided by the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.managementAction: Fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer was increased and copy of the bond was submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct periodic reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of said accounts.
observations.managementAction: Some balances are still for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer return immediately to the Bureau of Treasury the remaining Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) of the Municipality totaling ₱1,277,103.07.
observations.managementAction: Remaining balances still for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Require the reconstitution of the Inventory Committee with personnel who can handle the inventory-taking activity and instruct them to hasten the completion of the physical count of all the properties and prepare the necessary Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE). Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain property cards per category of property, plant and equipment and reconcile balances with the property, plant and equipment ledger cards that should also be maintained by the accounting department.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee started the physical count of properties and is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to prioritize the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements so as to reconcile the differences between the bank and general ledger cash in bank balances pursuant to Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. Likewise, enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the balances of their cash accounts as provided for under Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that since she assumed office, there was already a discrepancy between her records and the subsidiary ledger.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended that Management requires the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to determine the actual status of the each project so that adjustment could be made in the books of accounts in accordance with Section 50 Volume 1, of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to transfer said public infrastructures to the Registry of Public Infrastructures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant are coordinating to determine the actual status of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer dispose of the unserviceable motor vehicles and transportation equipment in the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 70 of PD 1445 and Section 125 of the NGAS, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Appraisal and Disposal Committee has started to dispose of the unserviceable properties but decided to conduct another auction because the prices quoted are too low compared to the appraised value.
observations.reason: For consideration in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine the actual creditors from whom withholding taxes were deducted and to require the Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld for the BIR. Moreover, the LGU should strictly adhere to the provision of Section 4(2) of Revenue Regulations No. 6-2001 dated July 31, 2001, otherwise, they shall be held liable for the penalties which may be imposed.
observations.managementAction: Taxes withheld including those of prior years were remitted to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: Create a committee that will determine the criteria/guidelines for undertakings such as the scholarship program and require the submission of an evaluation report to assess the viability and success of such undertakings.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines on the scholarship program were set.
observations.reason: No evaluation reports were submitted to the audit team yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Government of Basay adheres strictly to the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 in the utilization of the 20% Economic Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The ineligible nature expenses of AAR 2014 were no longer charged to 20% EDF in CY 2016.
observations.reason: Other nature expenses of same charged to 20% EDF in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Issue demand letters for the immediate liquidation of cash advances and after due notice, effect the withholding of all claims due the concerned officers and employees. b. Continue to monitor and control the grant of cash advances in previous cash in accordance with Sections 5.1, 5.7 and 5.8 of COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. c. Adhere and observe strictly the provisions of COA Circular No. 2003-003 dated July 30, 2003 regarding the liquidation and releases of cash advances of the intelligence fund.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the concerned official/employee.
observations.reason: Some of the withheld amount were refunded through salary deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and Treasurer to submit immediately the reconciled report on physical count of properties and all other reports required pursuant to DILG Memorandum Circular 2013-33.
observations.managementAction: Some documents were submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Some reports were not yet submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for additional appropriation to cover the full amount of the price of the lot purchased; b. Management submit the Deed of Absolute Sale and all the other supporting documents required under Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. c. The Mayor facilitate the transfer of title to the Municipality of Basay; and d. Henceforth, management desist from entering into a contract unless there is an appropriation therefore and from making advance payments on government contracts in accordance with Sections 85 and 88 of PD 1445, respectively.
observations.managementAction:  Additional funds were appropriated.  A Deed of Absolute Sale was already executed.  Titling is also processed.
observations.reason: may take time to finalize.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should require those no longer entitled to the TA to refund immediately the amounts claimed amounting to P74,250.00 and supporting documents such as DTRs and certification of the officials’ number of workdays of actual work performance for the month should be attached to the RA and TA payrolls.
observations.managementAction: Refund ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all employees concerned to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2013 and to henceforth observe strictly the applicable regulations on the grant of any fringe benefits.
observations.managementAction: Concerned officials and employees refunded through monthly salary deductions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Submit immediately the required supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers; b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations, COA Circular No. 2012-001, provisions in the Project Menu of the PDAF under GAA CY 2011-2013 and Sec. 4(6) of PD 1445 and suspension or disallowances; and c. Submit an explanation for the blatant disregard of procurement documentations/requirements, including the failure to conduct public bidding for vehicles purchased.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were submitted to COA for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a. Strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations, Sec. 4(6) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 as well as the special provisions/Project Menu in the GAA 2011-2013 on PDAF; b. Explain why no public biddings were conducted for the purpose; and c. Submit immediately all of the above stated supporting documents relating to the pre and post qualification requirements of RA 9184 that were not attached to the disbursement vouchers, including the following: i. Listing of medical missions conducted, its organizers, date, period covered, nature and target beneficiaries and list of medicines used, including its unit and total cost and duly signed receipt of such by end users. ii. Report of Inspection and Acceptance signed by proper agency officials. iii. Receipt of medicines and drugs by qualified beneficiaries both during the medical mission and other distributions made. iv. List of LGU personnel/officials who distributed the medicines and drugs and when/where did the distribution occurred. v. Procure drugs and medicines only from credible and qualified suppliers who have business establishments or pharmacies in the locality. vi. Explain the lack/absence of the required documents and why persons not connected with the LGU were allowed to receive the drugs and medicines in behalf of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were already submitted to COA, for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Municipal Accountant submit immediately all the documents required to support the disbursement vouchers covering the purchase of a fire truck as well as drugs and medicines together with an explanation for non- compliance with the circular. b. The BAC Chairman explain the failure to conduct the proper pre and post-qualification evaluations otherwise, if same was undertaken, submit the report duly signed by the BAC and TWG members. c. The BAC Chairman explain the absence of public bidding in the procurement of the fire truck. d. The concerned municipal officials such as the Mayor, Accountant, and BAC members, henceforth, comply with the procedural and documentary requirements of RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations, Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 which enumerate/provide the basic requirements and procedures in the procurement of goods through public bidding. e. The Municipal Treasurer issue the Property Acknowledgement Receipt to the officer/employee accountable for the fire truck. f. The LDRRMC strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 84 OF PD 1445 as well as Section 21 of Republic Act 10121 regarding LDRRMF and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 which provides for the accounting and reporting guidelines for the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Appropriate sufficient funds for Gender and Development equivalent to at least 5% of the agency’s total budget to various appropriations; b. Formulate a GAD Plan and Budget in accordance with the Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA and submit the same to the Philippine Commission on Women for approval; and c. Ensure that GAD programs and projects are given appropriate attention and implementation in order that GAD goals may be achieved.
observations.managementAction: GAD issues and concerns were mainstreamed to the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a review of the composition of the Accounts Payable account and determine their respective validity and effect the necessary reversion of those found not proper for the account; and b. In the future, conduct a regular review of all accounts payable on its propriety and validity to ensure that only legitimate claims are recorded in the books.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Accounts Payable is ongoing.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately remit the P28,158.00 to the concerned agency; and b. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to remit the exact amounts deducted and withheld from employees’ salaries to the PHILHEALTH.
observations.managementAction: Management is still tracing these amounts. The contributions are remitted exactly and promptly.
observations.reason: It would take time to trace the records of current prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Adopt measures to ensure the immediate implementation or liquidation of the financial assistance from the National and Local Government source agencies upon the completion of the intended purposes; b. To return any unused balances upon completion of the project/program. Otherwise, if there are priority projects that need additional funding, request for authority to use the balances; c. For unimplemented projects with huge fund allocation, evaluate whether the program/activities are still needed, if so, pursue its implementation as soon as possible; and d. Remit immediately to the concerned barangays their respective shares of 10% SK fund.
observations.managementAction: Some of the funds received from various source agencies like DOLE, DSWD and DILG were already liquidated.
observations.reason: Evaluation of some projects are still ongoing.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of the Implementing Rules of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid suspension or disallowances; and b. Submit immediately the supporting documents that were not attached to the disbursement vouchers.
observations.managementAction: Supporting documents have been submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Documents are still for evaluation.

For real property acquired prior to the creation of the municipality and for which records are no longer available, coordinate with the Provincial Register of Deeds and the Provincial Attorney’s Office for guidance in locating or applying for titles thereto.
